mnemonics: P-C-S: Parent Constructors Start first. Remember constructors execute in a Parent-to-Child Sequence.
keyPoints: A constructor for the root superclass is always executed first.
Each subsequent child class constructor executes after its parent's constructor is complete.
The final step is the execution of the constructor for the actual instantiated class.
commonMistakes: Assuming the constructor of the class being instantiated runs first.
Forgetting that the super() call is implicit in a constructor if not explicitly written, which still triggers the parent constructor chain.
